backend/main.mo: Main Motoko actor implementing the backend for a workplace access/resource request management system, including access control, user approval, user profiles, request lifecycle, audit logging, notifications, and analytics.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report mapping implemented features (visual theme, login page, dashboard, request wizard, etc.) to their corresponding source files, components, and test IDs with confidence ratings.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON document providing one-line summaries of each frontend source file, describing the purpose and notable implementation details of hooks, pages, components, and utilities.
frontend/index.css: Global Tailwind CSS entry point defining CSS custom properties for light and dark theme color tokens (background, foreground, primary, sidebar, chart colors, etc.).
frontend/index.html: HTML entry point for the React app titled 'Workplace Access & Resource Request Management System', mounting the app into a root div and loading the main TSX module.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root React app component that sets up TanStack Router with all six page routes (dashboard, create-request, request detail, approvals, reports, admin), handles authentication gating, and shows a profile setup modal when needed.
Client-side AI analysis panel component that evaluates a request's type, category, risk level, cost, and justification to display warnings, suggestions, an approval chain, and estimated completion time.
frontend/src/components/AIRiskPanel.tsx: Displays AI-generated risk and compliance flags for a request, checking for duplicates, high-risk system access, cost thresholds, and admin access, with color-coded severity indicators.
frontend/src/components/ApprovalActionBu: Renders Approve, Reject, and More Info action buttons for a request, each opening a confirmation dialog with an optional comments field that triggers the corresponding API mutation.
frontend/src/components/ApprovalPolicies: Shows existing approval policies and provides a form to add new ones by selecting request type, risk level, and required approver roles.
frontend/src/components/ApprovalTimeline: Renders a vertical timeline of approval steps for a request, showing each step's role, status, timestamp, and comments with color-coded icons.
frontend/src/components/AuditLogFeed.tsx: Fetches and displays a reverse-chronological audit log feed for a request, showing action type, actor, timestamp, and optional notes with icons per action.
frontend/src/components/ChatbotAssistant: A floating chatbot widget that parses natural language input to pre-fill a new request form, with example prompts and navigation to the request creation page.
frontend/src/components/KPICard.tsx: A reusable card component that displays a labeled KPI value with an icon, optional trend indicator, and configurable accent color.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: Main application layout component featuring a collapsible sidebar with navigation items, user profile dropdown, logout functionality, and a notification bell, with admin-only nav items conditionally rendered.
frontend/src/components/NotificationBell: Popover-based notification bell icon that displays unread count badge, lists notifications in a scrollable list, and allows marking individual notifications as read.
frontend/src/components/NotificationsTab: Admin tab component that lists all users with toggle switches to enable or disable in-app notification preferences per user, persisting changes via a mutation hook.
frontend/src/components/ProfileSetupModa: Modal dialog for first-time users to set up their profile by entering a full name and selecting a department, saving the data via a mutation hook.
Selectable card component for choosing a request type, displaying an icon, title, and description with highlighted styling when selected.
frontend/src/components/SLACountdown.tsx: Displays a live countdown to an SLA deadline, updating every minute and applying warning or overdue color styles based on remaining time.
frontend/src/components/SLARulesTab.tsx: Admin tab for configuring SLA deadline rules per request category, supporting inline editing of existing categories and adding new category rules with hour-based deadlines.
frontend/src/components/StatusBadge.tsx: Renders a styled badge for request statuses (pending, approved, rejected, etc.) and a separate RiskBadge for low/medium/high risk levels using predefined color configurations.
frontend/src/components/StatusStepper.ts: Renders a horizontal step indicator showing the progression of a request through statuses (Submitted → Under Review → Approved → In Fulfillment → Completed), with a special rejected state displayed as a red badge.
frontend/src/components/UserManagementTa: Displays a table of all registered users allowing admins to view and update each user's role via a dropdown, with save actions backed by mutation hooks.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Creates and caches an ICP backend actor (authenticated or anonymous) using React Query, reinitializing and invalidating dependent queries whenever the Internet Identity changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides an Internet Identity authentication context with login/logout functionality, identity persistence via AuthClient, and status tracking, exposed through a React context and hook.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Centralizes all React Query hooks for backend interactions including user profiles, roles, requests, audit logs, notifications, SLA rules, and approval policies.
frontend/src/index.css: Defines global styles, Tailwind base/component/utility layers, CSS custom properties for light/dark themes (navy/teal palette), and utility classes for request status badge colors.
frontend/src/lib/utils.ts: Exports a single `cn` utility function that merges Tailwind CSS class names using clsx and tailwind-merge.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that mounts the React app wrapped with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider.
frontend/src/pages/AdminConfigPage.tsx: Admin-only configuration page with four tabs (Users, SLA Rules, Approval Policies, Notifications) that renders an access-denied view for non-admins and includes a ChatbotAssistant.
frontend/src/pages/ApprovalsPage.tsx: Displays a filterable, searchable list of access requests; admins see all requests while regular users see only their own, with status/risk filters and approval action buttons.
frontend/src/pages/CreateRequestPage.tsx: Multi-step form (type selection, details, AI review) for submitting new access requests, with URL param pre-fill support from the chatbot and AI analysis panel integration.
frontend/src/pages/DashboardPage.tsx: Main dashboard showing KPI cards, pie/bar charts for request status and category breakdowns, and a recent requests table, scoped to the current user or all requests for admins.
frontend/src/pages/LoginPage.tsx: Login page with a branded left panel and an Internet Identity authentication button on the right, redirecting to the dashboard upon successful login.
frontend/src/pages/ReportsPage.tsx: Analytics and reporting page with filterable line, pie, and bar charts for request volume, approval rates, and SLA compliance, plus a paginated audit log with CSV export.
frontend/src/pages/RequestDetailPage.tsx: Detail page for a single request showing status stepper, metadata, SLA countdown, approval timeline, AI risk panel, audit log, and chatbot assistant. Fetches request data by ID and handles loading/error states.
frontend/src/utils/exportUtils.ts: Provides utility functions to export request data as a CSV file download and generate a plain-text print summary with approval statistics.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility functions for reading URL query parameters (supporting both standard and hash-based routing), persisting them to sessionStorage, and cleaning sensitive params from the URL hash.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration defining dark mode, custom color palettes (navy, teal, semantic tokens via oklch), typography, border radius, box shadows, and animation keyframes for the enterprise UI theme.
project_state.json: Project state manifest describing the full-stack workplace access/resource request management system (Team 23), including architecture notes, existing implemented features, known issues, and image requirements.
